name: Funds Payout Request
summary: Submits a funds payout (withdrawal) request for a logged-in user's account. POST to /FundsPayOutReq with a jData JSON body (uid, actid, payout, optional remarks) plus the session jKey; returns the transaction status, request time, and transaction id.

request_params.name: jData
request_params.description: Should send json object with fields in below list.

json_fields.name: uid
json_fields.description: User id of the logged in user.

json_fields.name: remarks
json_fields.description: Any message Entered during order entry.

response_fields.name: request_time
response_fields.description: This will be present only in a successful response.

response_fields.name: trn_id
response_fields.description: Tran id (transaction id). The response table row for this field is garbled in the source; the success sample uses the key trn_id.

notes:
- The curl sample uses placeholder 'https://BaseURL/'; the real base URL is https://piconnect.flattrade.in/PiConnectAPI (full endpoint https://piconnect.flattrade.in/PiConnectAPI/FundsPayOutReq).
- jData and jKey are passed as URL-encoded form data joined by '&' in the --data body (jData={...}&jKey=...), not as separate query string params.
- The curl sample's jData is malformed in the source PDF: it lists "actid" twice and omits both uid (a mandatory field per the table) and remarks; payout is shown empty. Treat it as illustrative only — per the field table the body should contain uid, actid, payout (all mandatory) and optional remarks.
- Response-field naming is inconsistent: the source response table calls the id row "Tran id" while the success JSON uses the key "trn_id". The table prose says request_time is present only in a successful response.
- stat value in the success sample is "W" (likely a pending/withdrawal-requested state); failure responses carry stat:"Not_Ok" plus an "emsg" error message (emsg is not documented in the response table).
- All "Possible value" cells in the request/json/response tables are blank in the source (no enums provided).
issues: The response table row for the transaction-id field is garbled in the cleaned text ("Tran status" / "Tran id" appear as stray lines after request_time); the page image confirms the response fields are stat, request_time, and a transaction-id field, and the success sample disambiguates the JSON key as "trn_id". The curl jData body is itself buggy in the source PDF (duplicate actid, missing uid/remarks, empty payout) — reproduced verbatim as required.
